item.title: 📰 Merger comms — COLA — Columbus Acquisition Corp/Cayman Islands — [425]
item.description:
Columbus Acquisition Corp (COLA), a SPAC, issued a $100,000 promissory note to WISeSat.Space Corp. to fund Monthly Extension Fees for its pending business combination with WISeSat.Space Holdings Corp. The note is convertible into private units at $10.00 per unit or into shares of the post-closing company at $5.00 per share. The business combination was announced on November 9, 2025, and the SPAC has until January 22, 2027 to close.

  • Counterparty: WISeSat.Space Holdings Corp.
  • Closing: by January 22, 2027

Mkt cap $85.3M · EV $57.8M · Financial Services / Shell Companies

COLA · 425 · cat=acquisition · filed 2026-05-11

item.title: ⚔️ Dissident — TWO — TWO HARBORS INVESTMENT CORP. — [DFAN14A]
item.description:
UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC) increased its acquisition proposal for Two Harbors Investment Corp. (TWO) to $12.50 per share in cash or 2.3328 shares of UWMC stock, urging TWO stockholders to vote against the pending merger with CrossCountry Mortgage (CCM) at the special meeting on May 19, 2026. UWMC claims its offer is clearly superior to CCM's $12.00 per share cash deal and intends to close within approximately two months of signing an agreement. The filing includes an open letter criticizing TWO's board for refusing to engage and for prioritizing management payouts.

  • Counterparty: UWM Holdings Corporation
  • Offer price: $12.50 per share in cash or 2.3328 shares of UWMC stock
  • Consideration: cash or stock election

Mkt cap $1.33B · EV $8.81B · Fwd P/E 11.1x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 2386.8x · EV/Sales 930.5x · Real Estate / REIT - Mortgage

TWO · DFAN14A · cat=activist · filed 2026-05-11

item.title: 📑 Proxy add'l materials — VSCO — Victoria's Secret & Co. — [DEFA14A]
item.description:
Victoria's Secret & Co. filed a supplement to its definitive proxy statement and sent a letter to shareholders responding to a proxy contest by BBRC International Pte Limited and its Chairman Brett Blundy, who seek to oppose the re-election of two board members at the upcoming annual meeting on June 11, 2026. The board urges shareholders to vote for all its nominees, citing strong performance and the risks of disruption. Director Mariam Naficy has decided not to stand for re-election due to the proxy contest.

  • Counterparty: BBRC International Pte Limited

Mkt cap $3.71B · EV $6.88B · Fwd P/E 13.2x · Fwd EV/EBITDA 9.6x · EV/Sales 1.0x · Consumer Cyclical / Apparel - Retail

VSCO · DEFA14A · cat=acquisition · filed 2026-05-11

item.title: 📋 8-K — INDI — indie Semiconductor, Inc.
item.description:
indie Semiconductor (INDI) announced a definitive agreement to acquire the CMOS image sensor business from ams-OSRAM AG for total consideration of €40 million (approx. $47.1 million), comprising €35 million cash at closing and a €5 million vendor note. The acquisition is expected to close in Q3 2026 and be immediately accretive, augmenting indie's ADAS and physical AI portfolio.

  • Counterparty: ams-OSRAM AG
  • Deal value: €40 million (approx. $47.1 million)
  • Consideration: cash
  • Closing: Q3 2026

Mkt cap $998.7M · EV $927.4M · EV/Sales 3.5x · Technology / Semiconductors

item.description:

item.description:
Odyssey Marine Exploration (OMEX) and American Ocean Minerals Corporation (AOMC) announced the filing of an S-4 registration statement for their previously announced all-stock merger. item.description:
Columbia Financial, Inc. announced regulatory approvals to acquire Northfield Bancorp, Inc. immediately upon completion of its second-step conversion from mutual to stock holding company. item.title: 📑 Proxy add'l materials — JUSHF — Jushi Holdings Inc. — [DEFA14A]
item.description:
Jushi Holdings Inc. is seeking shareholder approval at its June 24, 2026 annual and special meeting to reincorporate from British Columbia, Canada to Nevada via a plan of arrangement under Section 288 of the BCBCA. The arrangement would cancel the existing Canadian charter and adopt Nevada Articles of Incorporation and Bylaws, converting subordinate voting shares into Nevada common stock. The vote is scheduled for June 24, 2026.

item.title: 🤝 Material agreement — UMAC — Unusual Machines, Inc.
item.description:
Unusual Machines entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire Upgrade Energy for approximately $52 million, consisting of 1,792,012 shares of UMAC common stock valued at $13.9508 per share, $1 million cash at closing, and up to $26 million in earn-out cash payments based on revenue targets. The transaction is expected to close within 120 days, subject to customary conditions including delivery of Upgrade's 2025 audit.

  • Counterparty: Upgrade Energy (DroneNX LLC)
  • Deal value: $52 million
  • Consideration: cash+stock
  • Closing: within 120 days (approx. Q3 2026)

Mkt cap $594.2M · EV $230.8M · EV/Sales 7.9x · Financial Services / Shell Companies

item.title: 📋 8-K — CMPR — CIMPRESS plc
item.description:
Cimpress plc entered into a definitive agreement to acquire SAXOPRINT and viaprinto from CEWE for a net cash outflow expected to be less than €80 million after anticipated asset sales. The acquired businesses generated €89.6 million in revenue in calendar year 2025 with approximately 10% EBITDA margins. The acquisition is subject to antitrust approval and expected to close in the first half of Cimpress' fiscal year 2027 (July-December 2026).

  • Counterparty: CEWE Stiftung & Co. KGaA
  • Deal value: less than €80 million net cash outflow
  • Consideration: cash
  • Closing: first half of FY2027 (July-December 2026)

item.title: 📋 8-K — Enviri II Corp
item.description:
Enviri II Corporation (New Enviri) will be spun off from Enviri Corporation via a pro-rata distribution of one share of New Enviri common stock for every three shares of CE Holdings common stock. The spin-off separates the Harsco Environmental and Harsco Rail segments into a new publicly traded company, while Clean Earth is being sold to Veolia. The SEC declared the Form 10 registration statement effective on May 8, 2026, and the distribution is expected to become effective on June 1, 2026.

  • Closing: 2026-06-01
